Process ID of the SSH daemon handling the authentication event
Target user account for which publickey authentication was accepted
Source IP address from which the SSH connection originated
Source port number from which the SSH connection originated
SHA256 fingerprint hash of the accepted public key
Process identifier of the CRON session opener extracted from the square brackets.
The PAM service name used in the pam_unix authentication context.
The type of PAM operation being performed, such as session management.
The username for whom the session was opened.
The user ID of the account that initiated the session.
The hostname extracted from the log prefix identifying the source system
The process identifier of the CRON daemon handling the session
The PAM service module name used for authentication
The PAM type or action performed within the session
The event description indicating the closure of a user session
The username associated with the session being closed
Process ID associated with the SSH daemon handling the connection.
Source IP address from which the connection originated.
Source port number from which the connection was attempted.
Process ID of the su command, useful for event correlation and process lineage tracking.
The pseudo-terminal device associated with the user session, useful for session grouping and resource tracking.
The target user account for the su operation, indicating the user identity being switched to in the authentication event.
The authenticating user initiating the su command from the original session.
Process ID of the su command, useful for process lineage and logging entity identification.
The target user account for the su operation, used for access control and user session correlation.
The originating user account that performed the su operation, used for audit trails and identity mapping.
the user who executed the sudo command, useful for user-to-command mapping
the terminal device associated with the session, useful for session grouping
the present working directory when the command was executed, useful for understanding the execution context
the target user the command was run as, useful for privilege escalation tracking
the command executed via sudo, useful for command-line auditing and process lineage
The hostname or identifier of the server where the sudo session was closed
The process or program name associated with the authentication event
The PAM module used for the session management
The PAM service or context name for the session event
The username for which the sudo session was closed
The hostname of the machine where the log originates.
The name of the service or process generating the log entry.
The username of the user who executed the sudo command.
The terminal device associated with the user session.
The current working directory of the user at the time of the command execution.
The user identity that the sudo command is executed as.
The full command string executed with sudo, including arguments and flags.
Process identifier for the systemd-logind daemon generating the session event, useful for process lineage and entity alignment.
Unique identifier assigned to the newly created login session, used for session grouping and event correlation.
Authenticated username associated with the session, enabling user-level entity alignment and access tracking.
Process identifier of the systemd-logind daemon that managed the session.
Identifier of the user session that was removed.
